step_by_step_thinking:
Phenytoin, an anticonvulsant medication commonly used to treat seizures, can have several notable side effects. Among these, gingival hyperplasia (the abnormal overgrowth of gum tissue) stands out as one of its more characteristic adverse reactions due to increased collagen synthesis by fibroblasts.

My answer: A My clinical reasoning aligns with recognizing that phenytoin-induced gingival hyperplasia is well-documented and frequent among patients taking this drug for seizure management. The other options either do not correlate strongly or less frequently with phenytoin use compared to option A.
